nodes.label: AI as collaborative thinking partner for detective work
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable source requiring full verification
nodes.grounding_span: I will use a chatbot to help. I find that chatting about the problem usually either leads to the answer I'm looking for

nodes.label: Source traceability and trustworthiness
nodes.label_negative: Opaque AI outputs with unknown origin
nodes.grounding_span: I always check Google first because I can see exactly what site the information is coming from and discern whether it is a reliable source or not

nodes.label: AI as efficiency booster vs job security threat
nodes.label_negative: AI as cause of workforce reduction
nodes.grounding_span: I worry that at some point, they will start transferring people to other departments because there won't be enough work for us

nodes.label: AI use as workplace-sanctioned vs unsanctioned
nodes.label_negative: Unsanctioned AI use due to reliability concerns
nodes.grounding_span: It is generally not encouraged because it is not considered a reliable source

nodes.label: Epistemic certainty and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: I have to double check everything which takes longer

nodes.label: Job security and career stability
nodes.grounding_span: I worry that at some point, they will start transferring people to other departments

nodes.label: Efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: the program we use with the speech-to-text and other advances features are making the work much more efficient

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as thinking partner
nodes.grounding_span: I find it extra satisfying when I'm able to figure out the answer myself as I'm typing the prompt itself

nodes.label: Caution due to lack of source transparency
nodes.grounding_span: I don't know if the information is trustworthy (as I don't know the orignal source)

nodes.label: Anxiety about AI-driven workforce reduction
nodes.grounding_span: I worry that at some point, they will start transferring people to other departments

nodes.label: Pragmatic openness to future AI use
nodes.grounding_span: If our work were to change and include more writing and less editing, I might consider using AI to help formulate templates

nodes.label: Iterative problem articulation — uses writing to clarify thinking
nodes.grounding_span: just the act of writing it out and explaining the issue is enough for the puzzle pieces to fall into the right spots

nodes.label: Hierarchical source prioritization — prefers verifiable sources first
nodes.grounding_span: I always check Google first because I can see exactly what site the information is coming from
